module-3-clean-release-snapshot.label: Module Project: Clean the Volunteer Project Repository
module-3-clean-release-snapshot.summary: Finish volunteer-project with path cleanup, one ignore rule, safe recovery, and a clean handoff.

try_module_3_sketch4.title: Create and Recover a README Mistake

try_module_3_sketch5.title: Commit and Verify the Volunteer Project Handoff

try_module_3_sketch0.title: Add the Generated Volunteer Report
try_module_3_sketch0.prompt: Create `volunteer-project/generated/report.txt` with exactly three lines: `Generated volunteer report`, a blank line, and `Prepared automatically from assignments.` Save it, stage it, and commit it with the message `Add generated report`.
try_module_3_sketch0.hint: Record the supplied generated file once so you can later correct the tracking policy.

help.concept: This mirrors the guided project: a generated file can be committed, but a later ignore policy should keep regeneratable output outside future snapshots.
help.hint_1: Do not change the three existing project files.
help.hint_2: The commit should add only the report.

try_module_3_sketch0.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_module_3_sketch0.solutionCode:
git status --short
git add generated/report.txt
git commit -m "Add generated report"
try_module_3_sketch0.instructions: Create `volunteer-project/generated/report.txt` with exactly three lines: `Generated volunteer report`, a blank line, and `Prepared automatically from assignments.` Save it, stage it, and commit it with the message `Add generated report`.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
git -C volunteer-project init -q -b main
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Start volunteer project'
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM
' > volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Add volunteer meeting times'
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: Riley
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM
' > volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Update volunteer assignments'
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: Riley
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

Orientation: Saturday 9:30 AM
' > volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Update volunteer meeting time'
find volunteer-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Training desk: Riley

printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

git -C volunteer-project add -A

printf %s '# Volunteer Project

try_module_3_sketch1.title: Rename Assignments to Roles
try_module_3_sketch1.hint: Use the Git-aware move for the tracked path.

help.concept: `git mv assignments.txt roles.txt` renames the file and stages the path change together.
help.hint_1: Keep it in the repository root.

try_module_3_sketch2.title: Remove the Obsolete Meeting-Time File
try_module_3_sketch2.prompt: The orientation schedule has been moved elsewhere. Remove tracked `meeting-times.txt`, then inspect the staged name-status summary.
try_module_3_sketch2.hint: Stage the removal and review it with the rename.

help.concept: `git rm meeting-times.txt` deletes and stages the tracked path. `git diff --staged --name-status` shows the intended rename and removal before the final commit.

try_module_3_sketch2.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_module_3_sketch2.instructions: The orientation schedule has been moved elsewhere. Remove tracked `meeting-times.txt`, then inspect the staged name-status summary.

try_module_3_sketch3.title: Apply the Volunteer Ignore Policy
try_module_3_sketch3.prompt: Create `volunteer-project/.gitignore` with exactly `generated/` on line 1. Save it. Stage `.gitignore`, stop tracking `generated/report.txt` without deleting it, and inspect status with ignored paths included.
try_module_3_sketch3.hint: Add the policy, preserve the local report, and keep the earlier rename and removal staged.

help.concept: `git rm --cached generated/report.txt` removes the tracked relationship while preserving the working file. The `generated/` rule then classifies that local report as ignored.
help.hint_1: Do not stage the generated report again.
help.hint_2: The staged set should contain the rename, removal, `.gitignore`, and report untracking.

try_module_3_sketch3.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_module_3_sketch3.solutionCode:
git add .gitignore
git rm --cached generated/report.txt
git status --short --ignored
try_module_3_sketch3.instructions: Create `volunteer-project/.gitignore` with exactly `generated/` on line 1. Save it. Stage `.gitignore`, stop tracking `generated/report.txt` without deleting it, and inspect status with ignored paths included.

try_module_3_sketch4.title: Recover an Accidental README Rewrite
try_module_3_sketch4.prompt: Open `volunteer-project/README.md`. Find `Assignments and meeting times.` and replace it with `Accidental release rewrite.` Save the file. Stage the mistake, unstage it without losing the working copy, then restore the committed README. Finally inspect the staged name-status summary; only the intended maintenance work should remain.
try_module_3_sketch4.hint: Recover the staging layer first and the working-tree layer second.

help.concept: `git restore --staged README.md` removes the mistake from the proposal; `git restore README.md` then repairs the working file. Other staged paths remain untouched.
help.hint_1: Do not clear the rename, removal, or ignore-policy changes.
help.hint_2: The final staged summary should not include README.md.

try_module_3_sketch4.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_module_3_sketch4.solutionCode:
git add README.md
git restore --staged README.md
git restore README.md
git diff --staged --name-status
try_module_3_sketch4.instructions: Open `volunteer-project/README.md`. Find `Assignments and meeting times.` and replace it with `Accidental release rewrite.` Save the file. Stage the mistake, unstage it without losing the working copy, then restore the committed README. Finally inspect the staged name-status summary; only the intended maintenance work should remain.

try_module_3_sketch5.title: Commit and Verify the Volunteer Handoff
try_module_3_sketch5.prompt: Record the prepared maintenance snapshot with the message `Prepare volunteer project handoff`. Then inspect concise history, the newest commit summary, and final short status.
try_module_3_sketch5.hint: Commit the staged maintenance work and audit the handoff.

help.concept: `git commit` records the rename, removal, ignore policy, and generated-report untracking together. The history and status commands verify the final repository.
help.hint_1: The ignored report and preview remain local.
help.hint_2: Normal short status should be empty.

try_module_3_sketch5.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_module_3_sketch5.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Prepare volunteer project handoff"
git log --oneline
git show --stat --oneline HEAD
git status --short
try_module_3_sketch5.instructions: Record the prepared maintenance snapshot with the message `Prepare volunteer project handoff`. Then inspect concise history, the newest commit summary, and final short status.

moduleProject.description: Finish the same volunteer-project repository with one generated report, one rename, one removal, one ignore rule, and one safe recovery.
moduleProject.title: Prepare the Volunteer Project Handoff

intro-sketch.title: Finish the Volunteer Project Cleanly
intro-sketch.bodyMarkdown: The three-file volunteer repository now has focused history. Add one generated report, rename one tracked file, remove one obsolete file, introduce one ignore rule, recover one supplied README mistake, and finish with a clean handoff.
